description:
You are a mission analyst for the Mars Atmospheric & Climate Explorer (MACE). The spacecraft is on hyperbolic approach to Mars. Using the approach state and propulsion specifications in ~/Desktop/mars_capture_spec.txt, design the complete orbit capture sequence in GMAT.

The mission requires two impulsive burns at Mars periapsis: a Mars Orbit Insertion (MOI) burn that captures the spacecraft into a high elliptical orbit while simultaneously changing the orbital inclination, followed by a Period Reduction Maneuver (PRM) at the next periapsis to lower the apoapsis and achieve the target science orbit. Both burns must use differential correction targeting to achieve the specified orbital elements.

The GMAT simulation must use a Mars-centered coordinate system, Mars50c gravity model (degree >= 10), Sun third-body perturbation, and propulsion hardware with fuel depletion tracking. After achieving the science orbit, propagate for 30 Earth days to verify orbital stability.

All target orbital elements, spacecraft properties, and detailed burn strategy are specified in ~/Desktop/mars_capture_spec.txt.

Save the GMAT script to ~/GMAT_output/mars_capture.script
Save results to ~/GMAT_output/mars_capture_results.txt

Results format (one key: value per line):
MOI_DeltaV_mps: <value>
MOI_Fuel_kg: <value>
MOI_PostBurn_SMA_km: <value>
MOI_PostBurn_ECC: <value>
MOI_PostBurn_INC_deg: <value>
PRM_DeltaV_mps: <value>
PRM_Fuel_kg: <value>
PRM_PostBurn_SMA_km: <value>
PRM_PostBurn_ECC: <value>
PRM_PostBurn_INC_deg: <value>
Total_DeltaV_mps: <value>
Remaining_Fuel_kg: <value>
Stability_30day: PASS or FAIL

Login credentials: username=ga, password=password123
